summary: Umbau-Manager V2 is a German-language renovation/construction project management web app built for the Internet Computer (ICP). It uses Internet Identity for authentication and a Motoko backend canister to store per-user data for projects (with timelines and categories), tasks (Kanban workflow), documents (PDF uploads), media (images/PDFs with ordering and tags), contacts and helpful links, and project cost items with aggregated cost summaries. The frontend is a React SPA using TanStack React Query for data fetching/caching, PDF.js (via CDN) for previewing and thumbnailing PDFs, and a responsive layout with a collapsible sidebar, theme toggle, and reusable dialog/focus management utilities.
architectural_notes: Frontend is a single-page React application with internal page routing controlled by state (no external router) and cross-page navigation via a global CustomEvent('navigate').

TanStack React Query is used as the primary data layer (useQuery/useMutation) with explicit query keys, invalidation on mutations, and some refetch-on-mount behavior for documents/media to ensure immediate UI refresh after uploads.

ICP agent/actor lifecycle is encapsulated in a dedicated hook (useActor) that creates an authenticated actor when an Internet Identity is present, otherwise uses an anonymous actor. Actor creation calls backend.initializeAccessControl() to register roles.
Authentication uses @dfinity/auth-client (Internet Identity) with a React context provider, storing the delegation identity and supporting long TTL (30 days).

Backend is a Motoko canister that stores multi-tenant data in Maps keyed by Principal (projects/tasks/documents/media/contacts/links/cost items), enforcing ownership checks and role-based access control via an AccessControl module.

Blob/media/document storage uses a storage mixin (backend/blob-storage/Mixin.mo) and Prim storage APIs; includes gateway principal authorization refresh and cycles refill via a cashier canister principal from an environment variable.

PDF rendering is implemented with PDF.js loaded from a CDN; a global worker is initialized at the top of App.tsx to avoid race conditions. PDF preview uses a UnifiedPDFViewer (modal) and PDFThumbnail with sessionStorage-based caching.

Reusable UI patterns include BaseDialog (prevents accidental outside-click close, standard styling/scrolling), a centralized focusManager hook (useFocusOnMount / useFocusWithRestore), DeleteConfirmDialog, and ErrorBoundary for crash recovery.

Custom category values are managed client-side in localStorage (gewerke/bereiche/kategorien/media/document areas) and partially mirrored through backend mutations (createCustomCategory/deleteCustomCategory) for some category types.
PDF-Preview-Strategie für Medien: Option B gewählt -> Thumbnails server-/backendseitig einmalig erzeugen und persistent speichern (statisch 1. Seite), Viewer beim Klick bleibt separat/unchanged.
known_issues: Type safety gaps: multiple frontend queries/mutations call backend methods through `(actor as any)` (e.g., tasks, contacts, links, media update/bulk position updates, custom category operations). If backend signatures change or methods are missing, failures occur at runtime rather than compile time.

Backend/main.mo (as provided) includes projects, documents, media, cost items, profiles, and access control, but does not show task/contact/link/custom-category/bulk media position APIs that the frontend calls; this suggests either missing files or potential API mismatch risk.

ProfileSetupModal uses `autoFocus` on an input even though the project introduces a focus manager pattern and an ESLint rule to avoid autoFocus; this can reintroduce focus instability in nested dialogs.

PDF.js loading is duplicated: App.tsx injects the script globally, while UnifiedPDFViewer also attempts to load PDF.js if missing. This can cause redundant script insertion and makes load order more complex.
In-memory PDF caches (`pdfCache`, `pageRenderCache`) in UnifiedPDFViewer are unbounded and may grow over time (memory pressure in long sessions).
Task status changes fetch all tasks, find one, then update it (useChangeTaskStatus). This is inefficient and can be race-prone if concurrent updates occur.
Media grid image error handler replaces markup via `innerHTML` with inline SVG; while currently a static string, this pattern is generally risky if ever made dynamic.

AccessControl.getUserRole traps for non-anonymous principals that have not been initialized. The app mitigates this by calling initializeAccessControl during actor creation, but failures during initialization could lead to unexpected traps.
Recurring backend/actor API mismatch causes runtime failures like `actor.bulkUpdateMediaPositions is not a function` and breaks Media drag-and-drop persistence.
PDF preview thumbnails in Media grid still fall back to icons (no first-page preview), despite the requirement to implement persistent backend-generated thumbnails (Option B).

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication
existing_features.description: Provides login/logout via Internet Identity using @dfinity/auth-client with a React context provider that loads stored delegations on mount and supports a long-lived delegation (up to ~30 days).

existing_features.title: Backend actor initialization and access-control bootstrap
existing_features.description: Creates an ICP canister actor with or without identity, and calls backend.initializeAccessControl() to register/assign roles; invalidates and refetches dependent React Query caches when the actor/identity changes.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control (admin/user/guest)
existing_features.description: Motoko AccessControl tracks per-principal roles; first initializer becomes admin, subsequent principals become users; backend guards major APIs with permission checks and ownership validation.

existing_features.title: User profile management with first-run setup modal
existing_features.description: Fetches the caller’s profile and forces a profile setup modal for first-time users (name, optional email, user type privat/business). Saves profile to backend and uses it to adjust UI/behavior (e.g., customer field visibility).

existing_features.title: Responsive app layout with navigation, theme toggle, and user menu
existing_features.description: Implements a responsive layout with collapsible desktop sidebar, mobile off-canvas menu, dark/light theme toggle, and user avatar menu with logout.

existing_features.title: Project CRUD with timeline fields
existing_features.description: Create, list, edit, and delete projects with name, customer (or auto-filled for private users), color, start/end dates, category, and responsible contact. Backend stores projects per user and enforces ownership rules.

existing_features.title: Project categorization and category management
existing_features.description: Projects can be filtered by category; categories can be added via DynamicSelect (localStorage) and also created/deleted in backend via custom category mutations; supports renaming categories in UI with propagation to existing projects and updating category color.

existing_features.title: Roadmap views (list + calendar)
existing_features.description: Roadmap page supports a list view grouped by category and a calendar view (month/week) showing projects spanning days; projects are color-coded and can be expanded to show related tasks.

existing_features.title: Task management (Kanban board)
existing_features.description: Displays tasks in four status columns (Aufgaben, Diese Woche, Benötigt Feedback, Erledigt) with due-date sorting, urgency labeling, project/contact associations, and CRUD operations (create/edit/delete).

existing_features.title: Task filtering and quick status transitions
existing_features.description: Tasks can be filtered by project, trade (Gewerke), and area (Bereich). Tasks can be moved between columns via status-change actions; Dashboard provides quick actions for ‘Benötigt Feedback’ tasks.

existing_features.title: Dashboard overview with interactive navigation
existing_features.description: Dashboard shows key stats (projects, open tasks, completed tasks, feedback-needed) as clickable cards that dispatch navigation events to switch pages; includes upcoming deadlines and “this week” based on due dates.

existing_features.title: Calendar widgets for projects and tasks
existing_features.description: Dashboard calendar widget shows project start/end and task due events; clicking events dispatches navigation events to related pages.

existing_features.title: Document management with multi-file PDF upload
existing_features.description: Allows uploading one or multiple PDFs with per-file progress tracking and sequential uploads. Documents are organized into expandable folders by Bereich, with status/metadata display and delete confirmation.

existing_features.title: Media gallery (images and PDFs) with upload, search, and filters
existing_features.description: Supports uploading images and PDFs with progress, category assignment, tags, and list/grid view. Provides filtering by file type, category, and free-text search across name and tags, plus inline viewing.

existing_features.title: Drag-and-drop media reordering
existing_features.description: Media items can be reordered via drag-and-drop; the UI computes a new ordering and calls a backend bulk position update to persist positions, with subsequent query invalidation.

existing_features.title: Media metadata editing and deletion
existing_features.description: Allows editing media name/category/tags while preserving the original blob, and deleting media items with confirmation.

existing_features.title: Unified PDF viewer modal with caching and fullscreen controls
existing_features.description: Displays PDFs in a dialog with page navigation, optional download, keyboard shortcuts, fullscreen toggle, and zoom controls (zoom enabled only in fullscreen). Uses ArrayBuffer-based loading and caches PDF data and rendered pages for faster reuse.

existing_features.title: PDF thumbnail generation with persistent caching
existing_features.description: Generates first-page thumbnails using PDF.js by fetching PDFs as ArrayBuffer with Accept: application/pdf; caches thumbnails as DataURLs in sessionStorage keyed by a URL hash to speed up folder/media views.

existing_features.title: Contacts CRUD and search/filtering
existing_features.description: Manages contacts (name, company, role, email, phone, notes) with create/edit/delete flows, and UI filtering by search term and role.

existing_features.title: Helpful links CRUD with categorization
existing_features.description: Stores and manages helpful links with title, URL, category, description, and optional logo URL; includes searching/filtering and external link opening.

existing_features.title: Cost item management tied to projects
existing_features.description: Projects can be created with initial cost items; backend supports adding/updating/deleting cost items per project, retrieving all cost items, and summarizing paid vs open totals.

existing_features.title: Kostenübersicht page (cost overview) with grouping, filtering, sorting, and inline editing
existing_features.description: Displays all cost items grouped by project with per-project and global totals. Supports filtering by status, sorting by amount/date/description, inline editing of key fields, adding new cost items per project, and linking cost items to documents/media that can be opened in the PDF viewer.

existing_features.title: Blob storage mixin with authorization, cycles refill, and GC support
existing_features.description: Backend includes a storage mixin for managing ExternalBlob lifecycle, gateway principal authorization updates, dead blob enumeration and pruning confirmation, and a cashier top-up mechanism using cycles.

existing_features.title: Reusable dialog, focus management, and error recovery utilities
existing_features.description: Provides standardized dialogs that prevent accidental outside click close, consistent focus-on-open behavior via hooks, reusable delete confirmation dialogs, and an app-wide error boundary with reload recovery.

existing_features.title: Client-side custom category storage
existing_features.description: Persists and manages user-defined category lists (Gewerke, Bereiche, Kategorien, Media-Kategorien, Dokument-Bereiche) in localStorage with add flows integrated into DynamicSelect components across the app.

existing_features.title: URL/session parameter utilities for persisted parameters and secrets
existing_features.description: Utility functions to read parameters from query or hash, persist values in sessionStorage, and extract/clear secrets from the URL hash to reduce leakage risk.

existing_features.title: Backend support for media drag-and-drop ordering (bulk position update API)
existing_features.description: Adds/implements a backend actor method to persist media ordering via bulk position updates so the Media drag-and-drop reordering flow can complete without runtime actor method errors, with corresponding frontend typings/actor wiring updates.

existing_features.title: Persistent server-side PDF preview thumbnails for Media PDFs
existing_features.description: Implements persistent, one-time generated PDF first-page previews for Media PDF tiles (stored in backend/blob storage and reused), replacing unreliable inline rendering/fallback icons while keeping the full PDF viewer as a separate click-to-open experience.

existing_features.title: Fix Media deletion by implementing a backend `deleteMedia` API and wiring the frontend mutation to it so users can reliably delete images and PDFs without runtime errors.
Make PDF previews in the Media grid view render correctly and consistently (show a real first-page thumbnail preview instead of a generic file icon when the PDF is valid and reachable).
Extend the Roadmap project edit dialog to allow adding/editing/removing cost items directly in the editor, and ensure changes are synced with the Kostenübersicht page.
Make project start and end dates optional (can be left blank during creation and editing), and update Roadmap/calendar rendering to handle projects without dates gracefully.

new_feature_requests.summary: Medien-Reihenfolge/Sorting: Persistente globale Reihenfolge (nicht pro Projekt), kompatibel mit Filtern/Suche definieren und umsetzen
Media löschen fixen: Backend-API/Actor-Methode deleteMedia bereitstellen und Frontend-Mutation/Typing anpassen (Fehler: actor.deleteMedia is not a function)
PDF Preview im Media-Grid korrekt/funktionsfähig machen (Thumbnail/Preview-Darstellung)
Roadmap Projekt-Bearbeiten-Dialog erweitern: Kostenpositionen direkt im Editor hinzufügen/bearbeiten und mit Kostenübersicht synchronisieren
Projekte: Start- und Enddatum optional machen (später festlegbar) inkl. korrekter Darstellung in Roadmap/Kalender ohne Datum
